title: Input Actions, Bindings, Focus, and Keyboard Equivalence · Program Brief: Bind Scope Order
summary: Canvas clicks are the only navigation method, global key bindings fire inside text fields, focus becomes invisible, and repeat events race state changes. Predict the model, event, graph, solver, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the bind scope order increment in remappable action and focus-navigation map. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose widget, class, toplevel, tag, and bindtag scope while preserving expected default behavior.
Map keys, pointer gestures, buttons, and assistive commands onto named actions separated from solver state changes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

content.text: A worked example puzzle team handles case 8863 during a keyboard-only puzzle tournament. content.code:
# Worked focus: maze-bind-scope-order — Choose widget, class, toplevel, tag, and bindtag scope while preserving expected default behavior.
# Evidence: maze-worked-maze-input-focus-bindings-4
# Competency: maze-worked-maze-input-focus-bindings-4.
# Case 1655: a classroom algorithm comparison.
# Operating constraint: native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution.
# Changed case: change display scale and retain focus and control access.
def worked_maze_input_focus_bindings_4(actions: int = 9, keyboard_equivalents: int = 9, focus_stops: int = 7, trapped_stops: int = 0, ambiguous_bindings: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "gridworked4",
        "topology1655",
        "search4dekrow",
        "eventworked41655",
        "access1655worked4",
        "recovery4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if actions < 1 or keyboard_equivalents < 0:
        return False, keyboard_equivalents, "action-count"
    if keyboard_equivalents != actions:
        return False, keyboard_equivalents, "input-equivalence"
    if focus_stops < 1 or trapped_stops > 0:
        return False, trapped_stops, "focus-order"
    if ambiguous_bindings > 0:
        return False, ambiguous_bindings, "binding-scope"
    assert keyboard_equivalents == actions
    return True, keyboard_equivalents, "equivalent-input-map"
